critical_issues.issue: scrollbar-thin class is used but never defined
critical_issues.detail: ThinkingRow uses class='scroll-fade-mask mt-1 max-h-[300px] overflow-y-auto scrollbar-thin thinking-content'. 'scrollbar-thin' has no CSS definition anywhere in index.css or tokens.css — only 'scrollbar-none' exists. This is a silent no-op on non-Tailwind builds. The content scrollbar will render in default browser style (fat scrollbar) inside the narrow thinking drawer, looking ugly in every theme.
critical_issues.fix: Add .scrollbar-thin definition in index.css (scrollbar-width: thin; plus -webkit-scrollbar rules), or replace with scrollbar-none if the intent is to hide it entirely.

critical_issues.issue: Duplicate content-visibility declarations with conflicting contain-intrinsic-size values
critical_issues.detail: tokens.css applies 'content-visibility: auto; contain-intrinsic-size: auto 80px' to ALL [data-message-id] elements as a global CSS rule. message-row.tsx also sets these same properties inline with 'auto 120px'. The inline style wins due to specificity, but the global rule in tokens.css is now dead weight AND the two size estimates are inconsistent (80px vs 120px). The 80px estimate in tokens.css is never used. If the inline style is ever removed, it silently falls back to 80px causing layout shift. The duplication also signals intent confusion.
critical_issues.fix: Remove the [data-message-id] block from tokens.css entirely; keep only the inline style in message-row.tsx with the accurate 120px estimate. Or consolidate in CSS with a single accurate value.

critical_issues.issue: thinking-content class is used but never defined — potential styling gap
critical_issues.detail: The div wrapping rendered thinking markdown uses class='...thinking-content'. This class has no definition in any CSS file. If styles are ever needed for thinking content (e.g. spacing, code block overrides), this is the hook, but right now it silently adds nothing. Compare: 'message-content' class is defined and used for general markdown. The thinking content misses any .message-content overrides.
critical_issues.fix: Either add '.thinking-content' as an alias for '.message-content' in index.css, or replace the class with 'message-content' directly so code/pre blocks inside thinking get the same syntax highlight treatment.

critical_issues.issue: ToolCallGroup and ContextAwareToolList are exported but never imported anywhere
critical_issues.detail: grep across all .tsx files shows: only ContextGroupHeader is imported from ToolCallGroup.tsx (in MessageBubble.tsx and LiveStreamingBlock.tsx). The top-level ToolCallGroup component (the 'mega-group wrapper' for 3+ tools) and ContextAwareToolList are dead exports — they appear to have been superseded by the ToolSegmentDispatch pattern in MessageBubble. Dead exported components still get bundled and are confusing for maintainers.
critical_issues.fix: Remove or internalize ToolCallGroup and ContextAwareToolList exports if they are truly unused. Verify by searching for dynamic imports or test files before deleting.

warnings.detail: barColor() returns 'var(--success, #22c55e)'. This hardcoded #22c55e fallback will flash bright green in dark themes that define --success differently (e.g. mint/teal variants), and will not adapt to light mode which remaps --success. All other uses of --success in the codebase omit the fallback — this one is inconsistent.
warnings.fix: Remove the fallback: return 'var(--success)'. If --success could legitimately be undefined, add it to tokens.css instead of baking a hex value here.

warnings.issue: debugLog call inside a reactive Show condition creates a side effect in render
warnings.detail: The Show's 'when' accessor calls debugLog() inline: 'if (t) debugLog("thinking", "message metadata: yes", ...)'. This is called during SolidJS reactive tracking — every time the signal re-evaluates for any reason, a debug log fires. While gated by devMode, it's an anti-pattern that could mask reactive dependency bugs and produces noisy logs during streaming where the signal updates frequently.
warnings.fix: Move the debugLog call into a createEffect that tracks the relevant signal, so it fires intentionally rather than as a reactive side effect during rendering.

warnings.issue: isTruncated hint and error row can both render for the same message
warnings.detail: The 'Continue generation' button (isTruncated && isLastMessage) and the ErrorRow are both rendered in the same Show block without mutual exclusion. If a message has both props.message.error set and a content string that triggers the truncation heuristic (non-terminal-punctuation ending), both the error row AND the 'Continue generation' button appear simultaneously. The isTruncated memo does guard against 'props.message.error' but only for the heuristic calculation — the Show guard at L644 does not re-check error.
warnings.detail2: Confirmed: isTruncated() returns false when props.message.error is truthy (L369 guard exists), so the button is correctly suppressed. However the ordering is: isTruncated button first, then ErrorRow — if isTruncated() were ever true with an error (logic flaw), the button would appear above the error. Low severity but worth a defensive check.
warnings.fix: Add 'and !props.message.error' to the Show condition at L644 as a belt-and-suspenders guard.

warnings.issue: MessageActions is always rendered at position left:0 regardless of user vs assistant alignment
warnings.detail: The TimestampLine component renders MessageActions absolutely at 'absolute left-0 top-0'. For assistant messages this is fine (left-aligned). But TimestampLine is also called with align='right' for user messages (L484), and the MessageActions div is unconditionally placed at left:0 — so for user messages the actions would overlap the timestamp at the wrong end. In practice, MessageActions is only shown when props.message.content exists and !isStreaming, but the layout conflict still exists if a user message ever has actions.
warnings.fix: Conditionally apply 'right-0' instead of 'left-0' when align==='right', or guard the MessageActions render to only fire for assistant messages.

warnings.issue: O(n*m) diff algorithm with no input size guard
warnings.detail: computeLineDiff builds two Map<string,count> tables and iterates them. For tool outputs on large files (10K+ line rewrites), both Maps can hold thousands of entries. No size guard exists. The function is called synchronously in a createMemo during render. A single large write (e.g. regenerating a 20K-line file) will block the main thread during the memo evaluation.
warnings.fix: Add an early bailout: if oldContent.length + newContent.length > 200_000 (bytes), skip the diff and return {additions: 0, deletions: 0} with isNewFile logic only.

warnings.issue: window.innerHeight used inside JSX render for load-count calculation
warnings.detail: 'Math.min(Math.max(100, Math.min(400, Math.floor(window.innerHeight / 60) * 2)), data.hiddenMessageCount())' — window.innerHeight is read at render time and is not reactive. If the window is resized after initial render, the batch size does not update. Minor UX issue but window globals in JSX are a smell.
warnings.fix: Cache the computed batch size in a memo or move to a constant. Alternatively subscribe to window resize if dynamic batch sizing matters.

warnings.issue: Initial expanded signal captures anyRunning() at mount time but anyRunning is not reactive in createSignal
warnings.detail: createSignal(anyRunning()) at L157 reads the accessor once at component creation to set the initial value. This is correct for init, but the subsequent createEffect (L159-164) only responds to further changes. If the component mounts when anyRunning() is false but immediately a tool starts running before the next tick, the initial signal value is stale for one frame. Low probability but worth noting for correctness.
warnings.fix: This is acceptable SolidJS idiom. No action required unless flicker is observed.

warnings.issue: agent.thinkingSegments() called twice inside the same For loop render
warnings.detail: Inside the For each={agent.thinkingSegments()} at L277, the isLast check at L282 also calls agent.thinkingSegments().length. This means the signal is accessed twice per item in the loop — once for the For iteration and once for the length check. SolidJS will track both, creating two subscription paths for the same signal per item. A createMemo for the segment count would be cleaner.
warnings.fix: Extract 'const segmentCount = createMemo(() => agent.thinkingSegments().length)' and reference segmentCount() in the isLast expression.

suggestions.issue: color-mix(in srgb, ...) border has no fallback for Safari <15.4 / Firefox <113
suggestions.detail: LeadQuestionCard uses 'color-mix(in srgb, ...)' as a border style. color-mix() is broadly supported in modern browsers but Tauri's embedded WebKitGTK on Linux may lag behind. No @supports guard or fallback border is provided. If color-mix fails silently, the element renders with no visible border.
suggestions.fix: Add a fallback: set 'border: 1px solid var(--border-subtle)' first, then override with color-mix inside @supports (color: color-mix(in srgb, red, blue)) {}.

suggestions.issue: All three expandable headers missing aria-expanded attribute except ContextGroupHeader
suggestions.detail: TurnDiffSummary adds aria-expanded (L282). ContextGroupHeader does NOT. GroupHeader does NOT. ToolCallGroup wrapper does NOT. Screen readers cannot announce collapsed/expanded state for these interactive elements.
suggestions.fix: Add aria-expanded={expanded()} to the role=button div in GroupHeader and ToolCallGroup's wrapper header div.

suggestions.issue: <details> open attribute is not idempotent with SolidJS — use classList workaround
suggestions.detail: The native <details open={isOpen()}> works for initial render, but toggling the open attribute on a <details> element via DOM props in SolidJS can conflict with the browser's own toggle tracking when the user manually opens/closes it. After streaming ends (isOpen()=false), if the user had manually opened it, SolidJS will force-close it on the next re-render because open={false} forcibly removes the attribute. This breaks user intent.
suggestions.fix: Either use a controlled signal with onToggle handler (track user preference, only auto-close if user hasn't manually opened), or use a plain div with show/hide logic like the other expandable cards.

suggestions.issue: Assistant bubble width w-[90%] is fixed regardless of viewport — breaks at narrow widths
suggestions.detail: At 600px viewport width, the assistant message takes 540px leaving 60px margin. The user bubble uses 'max-width: min(85%, 68ch)' which is responsive. The assistant side uses a flat w-[90%] with no ch-unit cap. On very narrow screens the content can feel cramped. No @media queries anywhere in the CSS for message layout.
suggestions.fix: Consider matching the pattern: 'max-w-[min(90%,80ch)]' on the assistant container, giving a maximum character-width cap on wide screens.

suggestions.issue: compaction-pre opacity transition uses --duration-fast with fallback 150ms — inconsistent fallback value
suggestions.detail: The CSS reads: 'transition: opacity var(--duration-fast, 150ms) ease'. The actual --duration-fast is defined in tokens.css and resolves to a specific value. The 150ms fallback is fine, but other places in the codebase use 'var(--duration-fast)' without a fallback. Minor inconsistency.
suggestions.fix: Standardize: either always include fallbacks or never. Pick one convention.

suggestions.issue: Compact button onClick is async with no loading state or error handling
suggestions.detail: The Compact button fires an async handler that calls budget.compact() but has no try/catch, no disabled-during-loading state, and no user feedback on failure. If the operation throws (e.g. IPC error), it silently swallows the error. Double-clicking before completion could trigger two concurrent compactions.
suggestions.fix: Wrap in try/catch with a notification on failure. Add a local isCompacting signal to disable the button while in progress.

suggestions.issue: onBlur submits rename even when user clicks Cancel button
suggestions.detail: The rename input calls submitRename() onBlur. If the user clicks the Cancel button (Escape), the blur fires before the keydown, so submitRename() runs first with whatever the current renameValue() is, then the Escape keydown handler sets isRenaming(false). Depending on signal update order, this may submit the rename before canceling.
suggestions.fix: In the Escape keydown handler, set renameValue('') (or a cancellation flag) before blur fires, or use onBlur only when not cancelled (track a 'cancelled' ref).

suggestions.issue: Hardcoded 'ava is thinking...' brand string in component
suggestions.detail: The fallback typing indicator at L321 says 'ava is thinking...' as a hardcoded string with font-[var(--font-ui-mono)]. If the product is ever rebranded or this component is reused in a white-label context, this needs a prop or config reference.
suggestions.fix: Minor; acceptable as-is for now. Consider sourcing from a config constant.

dead_code.detail: Exported but never imported anywhere in the codebase. Superseded by ToolSegmentDispatch in MessageBubble.tsx.
Exported but never imported anywhere in the codebase. Both the component and its ContextAwareToolListProps interface are unused.
Internal helper used only by ToolCallGroup which is itself dead. If ToolCallGroup is removed, toolSummary goes with it.

The prop is declared in the interface and passed from toolbar-strip.tsx, but is never read inside StatusBar's implementation. The body of the component never references props.streamingTokenEstimate. Dead prop.
Overridden in every case by the inline style in message-row.tsx which sets the same properties with a different (correct) size of 120px. The CSS rule is never effective.

summary: The codebase is in good shape overall — the architecture is sound, the CSS custom properties are properly themed, and the reactive patterns are mostly correct. Four issues need fixing before ship: (1) 'scrollbar-thin' is used but undefined, causing a fallback to ugly native scrollbars in ThinkingRow; (2) 'ToolCallGroup' and 'ContextAwareToolList' are dead exported components that add bundle weight; (3) 'content-visibility' is declared twice with conflicting values; (4) the 'streamingTokenEstimate' prop is declared and passed but never consumed. The most risky runtime issues are the TurnDiffSummary O(n) diff with no size guard (a large file rewrite can block the main thread), the Compact button having no error handling or double-submit guard, and the details/summary open-attribute conflict with user interaction in ThinkingRow. Accessibility gaps: missing aria-expanded on GroupHeader and ToolCallGroup. No mobile breakpoints exist anywhere — the app is clearly desktop-first (Tauri), so this is acceptable by design.
